title: Reduce Dockerfile based build time for libpod.
body:
libpod code added at end of Dockerfile, avoids
git clone of other packages in Dockerfile on subsequent builds.

Signed-off-by: Kunal Kushwaha <kushwaha_kunal_v7@lab.ntt.co.jp>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *kunalkushwaha*, *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

The changes LGTM on the surface, but the info tests don't look happy with this change.

```
/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/info_test.go:38
```

Not sure, why these test failed. 

I guess another PR https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2257 , which do not change any code also fails on `podman-info` tests

@kunalkushwaha: Cannot trigger testing until a trusted user reviews the PR and leaves an `/ok-to-test` message.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2253#issuecomment-460186605):

>Not sure, why these test failed. 
>
>I guess another PR https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2257 , which do not change any code also fails on `podman-info` tests


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

@kunalkushwaha and @TomSweeneyRedHat you're right to re-run the failing test.  If it breaks again, it's worth looking for the actual cause of the failure (download the logs and grep/search them).   The majority of the time, you'll see it's something outside outside of our control.  e.g. network service timeout, host unreachable, or some other external service.  If not, or if you need help determining, please let me know, I'm happy to take a peek.
